business.name: Reservoir
business.positioning: Reservoir tells homeowners exactly how much cash to keep on hand so a dead furnace is an annoyance, not a crisis.
business.targetAudience: New and recent homeowners (first 5 years) who were surprised by major-system and appliance repair costs and want a defensible plan instead of dread.

designBrief.recommendedStyle: Steady, editorial, instrument-panel calm. Reads like a trusted financial dashboard, not a chore tracker or a game. Generous whitespace, one clear number per screen, quiet confidence.
designBrief.paletteMood: Deep navy as the steady anchor, warm paper background, a single confident amber used sparingly for the one thing that needs attention. No red-alert dread; risk is shown as managed, not alarming.
designBrief.typographyMood: Warm serif display (Fraunces) for the headline number and section anchors; clean neutral sans (Inter) for everything operational. Numbers are the hero typography.
keyEffects: The Reserve Number counts up to its target once, calmly, on first reveal
A coverage meter that fills toward 'covered' rather than draining toward 'doom'
Soft state transitions; nothing flashes, buzzes, or celebrates with confetti
antiPatterns: No mascot, no cute characters, no anthropomorphized appliances
No streak guilt, fake urgency, or countdown timers on financial decisions
No alarmist red 'YOU ARE AT RISK' framing; no gamified points/badges
No dark-pattern paywall that hides the user's own inventory behind the wall
designBrief.motionNotes: Web surfaces animate with framer-motion from the motion.* tokens; the mobile binary uses native animation; honor reduced motion. Motion communicates steadiness (ease-out settles), never excitement.
designBrief.notes: Run `npm run seed:design-brief`, then the ui-ux-pro-max skill, to refine recommendedStyle, paletteMood, typographyMood, keyEffects, and antiPatterns. Reference-only: adapt its guidance, do not paste its data. Brand intent is locked by the founder: calm, defensive, non-gamified, no mascot.

versionLog.summary: Seeded Reservoir Design Room from the skill template: brand name, positioning, target audience, preparedness theme tokens (navy/amber/paper), and the V1 core screens/flows for the appliance inventory and Reserve Number.

Renamed the working codename Ballast to Reservoir (founder selection). Chosen for visualizable readiness: the reserve's fill level mirrors the app's coverage meter, so brand and core UI share one gesture. Legal/availability clearance still pending; see NAMING.md.

landingPages.name: Reservoir waitlist landing
landingPages.purpose: Pre-launch waitlist: state the surprise-cost problem, the Reserve Number promise, and capture email. AI-search visible.
decisions: Lead with the homeowner surprise-cost pain, not features
One promise above the fold: know your number before something breaks

screens.name: Appliance inventory onboarding
screens.purpose: Walk the homeowner through inventorying major systems and appliances (HVAC, water heater, roof, garage door, kitchen, laundry) with age and rough value, building toward the value reveal.
decisions: Inventory is the value reveal, not a data-entry chore
Smart defaults for typical replacement costs so users can move fast and refine later
Native App Review prompt fires immediately after the first Reserve Number reveal

screens.name: Reserve Number (home dashboard)
screens.purpose: The hero screen: the single recommended home emergency-fund figure as a percentage of total replacement value, plus a coverage meter showing how covered the user is today.
decisions: One big number is the hero; methodology is one tap away, never hidden
Coverage meter fills toward 'covered', never drains toward 'doom'
The Reserve Number itself is free; depth/probability/recommendations are paywalled

screens.name: Item detail & spend log
screens.purpose: Per-item record: model, age, replacement estimate, spend-to-date, manuals, recommended spare parts to keep on hand, and the trusted pro assigned to it.
decisions: Track money spent per item to show real cost of ownership over time
Manuals and recommended-parts links reduce time-to-resolution for techs

screens.name: Trusted people & pros
screens.purpose: The homeowner's roster of trusted people/pros (HVAC tech, plumber, electrician, handyman) attached to the items they cover.
decisions: Pros are attached to items, so when something breaks the next call is obvious

screens.name: Reserve+ paywall
screens.purpose: Soft paywall presented after the free Reserve Number reveal: unlocks failure-probability ranking, what-breaks-first ordering, recommended parts-on-hand, and ongoing recommendations.
decisions: Paywall appears after value is shown, not before
Never hide the user's own inventory data behind the wall

flows.name: First session: inventory to Reserve Number
steps: Inventory major systems and appliances with age + smart-default values
Reveal the recommended Reserve Number (% of replacement value)
Soft Reserve+ paywall for probability and recommendations

flows.name: Manage an item over time
steps: Log a repair/spend or update age
Attach manuals, spare parts, and a trusted pro
See the Reserve Number and coverage update

defaultProductPage.positioning: Know your home emergency fund before something breaks.
